question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gas value of exactly 10,001 (just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR=10,000) so that scaleGas() returns only 1 token consumed from gasLimitBucket, allowing the attacker to submit nearly 10,000x more gas-equivalent work per token than intended, effectively bypassing the gas-per-second budget?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket is a single global shared bucket (no per-IP or per-user isolation) to flood the server with requests up to the global requestsPerSecond limit, starving all other legitimate users and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas=21,000 (the minimum allowed by @Min(21_000)) so that scaleGas(21_000)=2 tokens are consumed per request, allowing the attacker to exhaust the rateLimitBucket (requestsPerSecond=500) before the gasLimitBucket is meaningfully depleted, effectively bypassing the gas throttle entirely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check order in throttle() — rateLimitBucket checked before gasLimitBucket — to consume a rateLimitBucket token without consuming any gasLimitBucket tokens by sending gas values that scaleGas() maps to 0 (gas <= 10,000), causing asymmetric bucket depletion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) is checked first and, if it succeeds, gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() is checked second — but no rollback of the rateLimitBucket token occurs on gasLimitBucket failure — to gradually drain the rateLimitBucket while the gasLimitBucket is already exhausted, causing legitimate users to be rate-limited even when gas capacity is available?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated failed contract calls (which cause restore() to be called via ContractCallService.restoreGasToBucket()) to artificially inflate the gasLimitBucket beyond its intended capacity, allowing subsequent high-gas requests to bypass the gas-per-second throttle?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a negative gas value to restore() (since the method signature accepts a raw long with no lower-bound validation), causing gasLimitBucket.addTokens() to be called with a negative value and potentially corrupting the bucket state or throwing an uncaught exception?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition `if (tokens > 0)` in restore() — where scaleGas(gas) returns 0 for gas <= GAS_SCALE_FACTOR — to cause restore() to silently do nothing even when gas was consumed, leading to permanent token loss from gasLimitBucket and a self-inflicted denial-of-service on the gas throttle?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single request to the opcode endpoint to exhaust the opcodeRateLimitBucket (default capacity=1), blocking all other users from accessing the opcode tracing endpoint for the entire refill period (1 second), constituting a trivial single-request denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that opcodeRateLimitBucket is not created with SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (unlike gasLimitBucket) to race concurrent requests through the opcode throttle check, allowing more than opcodeRequestsPerSecond requests to pass simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with a user-controlled `data` field containing CRLF sequences or log-format injection payloads, which are then logged verbatim via `log.info(\

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with an extremely large `data` field (e.g., megabytes of hex data) that, when matched by a LOG-action filter, causes log.info() to write enormous log entries, exhausting disk space or log buffer memory and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the THROTTLE action branch where filter.getBucket().tryConsume(1) uses a per-filter bucket with capacity=rate (from RequestProperties.createBucket()), and if rate=100, send 100 matching requests per second to exhaust that per-filter bucket while the global rateLimitBucket still has capacity, causing selective throttling of specific request patterns?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the for-loop over throttleProperties.getRequest() — which evaluates all filters sequentially — to craft a request that matches multiple THROTTLE-action filters simultaneously, consuming tokens from multiple per-filter buckets in a single request and exhausting all filter-specific throttle budgets faster than intended?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the for-loop in throttle() does not break after the first matching filter, allowing a single request to trigger multiple action() calls (e.g., LOG + THROTTLE + REJECT in sequence), causing unexpected behavior such as logging before rejection or consuming a THROTTLE bucket token before throwing a REJECT exception?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests at exactly the rateLimitBucket refill rate (requestsPerSecond tokens per second) in a sustained manner to keep the bucket perpetually near-empty, causing legitimate burst traffic from other users to be throttled while the attacker's steady stream passes through?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the greedy refill strategy of rateLimitBucket (refillGreedy(rateLimit, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) to send bursts of up to requestsPerSecond=500 requests instantaneously at the start of each second, overwhelming downstream processing even though the per-second average is within limits?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() truncation (Math.floorDiv(gas, 10_000)) to send gas values just below multiples of 10,000 (e.g., gas=19,999 → 1 token, gas=29,999 → 2 tokens) to maximize gas-per-token efficiency, consuming far less gasLimitBucket capacity than the actual gas value represents?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-source-IP or per-client-identity throttling in throttle() to monopolize the entire global rateLimitBucket from a single connection, denying service to all other users sharing the same global bucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket uses SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED while rateLimitBucket does not, creating a race condition where two concurrent threads both pass the rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) check before either reaches the synchronized gasLimitBucket check, allowing more requests per second than rateLimitBucket intends?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where restore() is called with gas=Long.MAX_VALUE (if upstream validation is bypassed), causing scaleGas(Long.MAX_VALUE) to return Long.MAX_VALUE/10_000 = 922_337_203_685_477, and gasLimitBucket.addTokens() to add an astronomically large number of tokens, effectively disabling the gas throttle?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttle() consumes from gasLimitBucket using the request's gas field (user-controlled) rather than actual gas used, to send requests with gas=15,000,000 (max) to rapidly exhaust the gasLimitBucket and deny service to all other users for the remainder of the refill period?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any authentication or session binding in throttle() to distribute requests across many source IPs (botnet/distributed attack) and collectively exhaust the global rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket, causing a distributed denial-of-service with no per-client mitigation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: '] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RequestProperties.test() uses a probabilistic rate check (RandomUtils.secure().randomLong(0L, 100L) >= rate) for non-THROTTLE actions, meaning that with rate=1, only ~1% of matching requests trigger the action — allowing an attacker to send 100x more matching requests before the action fires, effectively bypassing LOG or REJECT filters statistically?
